llm_verdict.message:
The user initialization files have been remediated:

* All common shell start‑up files (`.bashrc`, `.bash_profile`, `.profile`, `.cshrc`, `.kshrc`, `.login`, `.zshrc`, `.zprofile`, `.bash_aliases`, `.bash_logout`, `.zlogin`, `.zlogout`) located in **/root** and every user’s home directory have had their permissions set to **0740** (owner read/write/execute, group read, others none).
* Files under any `.profile.d/` directories have also been examined and, where necessary, set to **0740**.
* A verification search for any group/other write or execute bits on these files now returns no results, confirming the permissions are correctly applied.

The OpenSCAP finding **“Ensure All User Initialization Files Have Mode 0740 Or Less Permissive”** is now resolved.
